ops.title: Yasyf runs several concurrent Claude sessions per repo; treat shared working copies as live and origins as moving targets
ops.body:
Yasyf's repos (cc-present, cc-context, captain-hook, cc-skills) routinely have another live Claude session editing the shared working copy and pushing to origin mid-task — releases can be cut underneath you (captain-hook went 8.0.0, then 8.1.0, then 8.2.0 by a parallel session during one 2026-07-03 work session, so the planned tag became 8.3.0).

**Why:** Two agents assuming they own the checkout clobber each other; a rebase target can move between fetch and push.

**How to apply:** Never park or commit another session's uncommitted edits. Do rebases, conflict resolution, and test runs in an isolated `jj workspace add` (jj-colocated repos) or `git worktree`; pure history operations (`jj rebase -s`, `jj bookmark set`) are safe and carry the live session's `@` along as a descendant. Fetch immediately before any push and re-rebase rather than force. Verify a "release already exists" surprise (`git ls-remote --tags`, registry JSON) before picking the next version. The cc-notes task store also gets reconciled/pruned by hooks in these repos — re-check a filed task still exists before relying on it. Related: [[publish-via-ci-only]].
